TextENGNew: ``...Ahhh, I can't take it!  How unpleasant — truly, utterly unpleasant!  ...Jirou-san, fawning all over a girl that much younger than him!``
``I mean, yes, I suppose I'm not exactly forthcoming with my feelings!  Whenever he asks me for something, I want to refuse at first, just to tease him!``
``...But I wish he'd understand — it's only because I genuinely like him that I act that way!``
``I mean, if Jirou-san really begged me for it, I wouldn't mind modeling for him in my work clothes, but... ahhn, it's just so embarrassing!``
``But if he truly, absolutely insisted, then — hiyeeeek?!``
``Did you hear all that?!``
``No, I didn't hear anything.``
``Wh-What is it?  Did you need something?  If it's about Jirou-san, I don't want to hear it.``
``...Who are you angry with?  Is it Rena?  Or is it Tomitake-san...?``
``It's nothing to do with you, Rena-chan.  I was just exhausted by how spineless he is.``

``That's exactly right.  Rena was exhausted, too.  He gets to spend all that time with someone as charming as Takano-san, and then he goes and gets distracted by another girl? The absolute worst.  ...To be unable to see how wonderful you are, Takano-san — what a pitiful man.``
``Is... is that so?  Ho ho ho ho!``
``Oh please, I don't want to hear that from someone who butted in!@k@vS07/09/330900055. Besides, what did you do with Jirou-san?@k@vS07/09/330900056. Why did you follow me?``
``Hau.@k@vS07/02/330200142. I don't care about Tomitake-san at all.@k@vS07/02/330200143. And Rena... actually, well, um......``
``Hm?@k@vS07/09/330900058. What is it?@k@vS07/09/330900059. Could you say that a little louder?``
``The one Rena truly loves is you, Miyo-san!!``
``Wh-Whaaaaaat?!@k@vS07/09/330900061. Wait-- ho, ho ho ho ho!@k@vS07/09/330900062. You shouldn't tease adults like that, Rena-chan...``

``What does being an adult have to do with it?@k@vS07/02/330200146. Am I not allowed to love someone just because I'm not an adult?@k@vS07/02/330200147. Isn't it by loving someone that you become an adult...?``
``U-Um... ho ho ho ho ho ho...!@k@vS07/09/330900064. Hey, don't cling to me like that...!@k@vS07/09/330900065. Is this some kind of punishment game?!``
``A punishment game? Absolutely not!!@k@vS07/02/330200149. Rena has always had eyes only for Miyo-san!``

``Ahhhhh, I want to call Miyo-san 'onee-sama'!@k@vS07/02/330200151. I want to be Miyo-san's little sister!!@k@vS07/02/330200152. I want Miyo-san to fix my tie every morning and say 'Your tie is crooked!'!!``
``Y-You know, people get the wrong idea sometimes, but I really don't have those kinds of interests at all...``
``That's a lie!!!``
``Rena can tell!!``

``I see the way Miyo-san looks at men with those disgusted eyes!@k@vS07/02/330200156. Your relationship with Tomitake-san might look romantic on the surface, but it's really just self-deception!@k@vS07/02/330200157. You're only doing it to fool yourself into thinking you can have a normal relationship!!``

``I'm sure Miyo-san has started to realize it deep down, hasn't she?!@k@vS07/02/330200159. Men are nothing but beasts!@k@vS07/02/330200160. There's no way a person and a beast could ever truly understand each other.``
``But... Rena and Miyo-san can surely come to understand each other!!``

``I want to unravel the deep mysteries of Onigafuchi Village with Miyo-san.@k@vS07/02/330200163. I want to do a real Watanagashi with Miyo-san, and sneak into the ritual storehouse together to investigate and uncover the secrets of Hinamizawa's past... with you...``
By the time we rushed over, Rena was in the middle of spouting the most outrageous pick-up lines.

You've got to be kidding me! If she teamed up with occult-obsessed Takano-san, 'Keiichi-kun's fur seal is so kyuuute~! That's Hinamizawa's true treasure! Hau~, I'm taking it home!' -- she'd totally do it for real, and that's not even funny!!
``Ahh, ahahahaha!@k@vS07/03/330300072. So sorry about this, Takano-san!@k@vS07/03/330300073. It seems spring has arrived inside Rena's head, and well~``
``...Spring?@k@vS07/09/330900068. What do you mean, spring?``
``Oh, haha, well you see!@k@vS07/01/330100138. Everything Rena just blurted out has nothing to do with what she actually wants, and...``
``Silence, you male ape.``

``Ahhh, Rena-chan!@k@vS07/09/330900071. I've finally realized it!@k@vS07/09/330900072. Yes, yes, that's what it was!@k@vS07/09/330900073. Thanks to you, I finally understand what was missing when I was with Jirou-san!``
``Hau, Miyo-san...``
``Hey, hey, no hugging allowed!!``
``Bghyabgbrpah?!``
...Rena's full-force blow connected dead-on, and Satoko went flying spectacularly.
``...Rena didn't even show mercy to Satoko!``
``Hau~, even if it's Satoko-chan, Rena won't forgive anyone who tries to tear apart our true love~!``
``Geez... this is even worse than the Tomitake situation, since she's actually welcoming it!``

``Thank you, Rena-chan!@k@vS07/09/330900075. I feel like I could stay with you forever!@k@vS07/09/330900076. Now, let's go open the forbidden door together!@k@vS07/09/330900077. And then, under divine blessing, we'll remain side by side for all eternity!!``
``Ahhhn, Miyo-saaan!@k@vS07/02/330200167. Um, would it be all right if I called you 'onee-sama' from today on...?``
...Oh, give me a break!! At this rate, the most terrifying power couple will be born by tomorrow night!
They'll take my fur seal home and... th-th-this is no joke!!
Just then, Satoko finally came back to life and staggered to her feet. ...The red tissues stuffed up her nose looked utterly pathetic.
``Rikaaa!@k@vS07/04/330400158. Takano-san definitely has the magatama, right?!``
``...Yes, she does!!@k@vS07/05/330500129. If this keeps up, a catastrophe might befall this village!``

``...Mion-san, Keiichi-san, Shion-san!@k@vS07/04/330400160. Rika says that if we leave things as they are, it won't just be Rena-san's life at stake -- something terrible could happen to all of Hinamizawa!!``
``We need to get the magatama back from Takano-san as soon as possible!``
``Indeed!!@k@vS07/06/330600048. Takano-san, that white seal keychain you have -- that belongs to Rika-chama!@k@vS07/06/330600049. Could you please return it?``
``I don't want to.@k@vS07/09/330900079. Why should I give away something someone gave to me?@k@vS07/09/330900080. But wait, this was Rika-chan's?``
As she said this, Takano-san pulled the white magatama from her pocket and examined it with great interest.
``That's right!@k@vS07/03/330300076. It's apparently a very important treasure of the Furude Shrine!``
``Hey, you idiot Mion!@k@vS07/01/330100140. If you tell Takano-san that, she'll...!``

``What did you say?@k@vS07/09/330900082. A precious treasure of the Furude Shrine...?@k@vS07/09/330900083. Tee hee, oh ho ho ho ho ho!!@k@vS07/09/330900084. Now that I know that, there's no way I'm giving it back easily!``

``Come along, Rena-chan!  The two of us are going to conduct a thorough investigation of this mystical jewel!  Ahh, what shape is it?  What weight, what size?!  Ahhn, maybe I should see how it tastes as well!!``
``Ah, the taste is terrible — get her!!``
``Ohohohohohoho!  No, no, no — I won't be giving this back~!``
``Hey!  Stop right there!!``
``Hau — no you don't!  Rena won't let anyone chase after Miyo-san!!``
Rena threw her arms wide and blocked us before we could follow Takano-san!
...Against anyone else, that wouldn't have been much of an obstacle.  But with Rena, it looked like real trouble.
...Actually, the way Rena was right now, I had the feeling even tanks and battleships wouldn't be enough!
``D-Damn...!  Rena, get out of the way!!  We just want that magatama Takano-san ran off with!!``
``No.  Not a single soul shall interfere with Miyo-san's research — not while Rena Ryuugu still draws breath!!``
``Gah — in close quarters her reach and speed are both way above ours!``
``Which means we have to attack from outside her range!!  Sorry, Rena — please don't hold this against me!!``
Mion pulled from inside her jacket — a pair of pistols?!

No, wait — those were model guns.  The ones she used to carry around in casual clothes, as if they meant something, except she never once drew them in the main story despite all the buildup, until the whole subplot had to be quietly dropped!
``Kids, never point a weapon at another person like I'm doing right now!!  Eat my Mion Special!!``
With breathtaking form, she pelted Rena with a barrage of airsoft pellets!
Her marksmanship was unmistakably modeled after that certain style — the one where mastering even the basics would push your combat ability to 120%!!
But Rena didn't move a single step.  Swatting the air with both hands, she deflected every single pellet at point-blank range!!
``Oh?  Was that your trump card, Mii-chan?``
Rena smiled — sharp and cool — and turned both palms toward us.
Then, fingers moving like a stage magician rolling a coin across his knuckles, she twirled every caught pellet between her fingers.
``Shooting a model gun at someone from this close... I'm not very impressed with you.  Not very impressed at all.``
``Hmm?  What's the matter, Kei-chan — does your stomach hurt?``
``I-it's nothing...``
``Well then, Rena's turn!  Rena's turn, Rena's turn!!  Anyone who gets in the way of Rena's romance will find themselves in a very sorry state~!!  Hau hau hau~!!``
``N-Now's your moment, Keiichi-san!  Use that silver tongue of yours on Rena-san and put an end to this!  Just like you did with me!``
``That's easier said than done!!  That's a secret art within secret arts — even I can't just pull it out whenever I like!``
``So secret that in ancient China it was feared as the ultimate forbidden technique — one that kills the very person who uses it.  Martial artists wagered their lives to employ it just once!!``
``Though, when I think about it, that kind of technique gets used quite a few times in your average fighting manga, doesn't it?``
``And that's where the theory of 'lives remaining' comes in.``

``We say that people only have one life — yet at the same time we accept that cats have nine, and there's no shortage of classical Japanese literature that understands the concept of lives remaining.``
``In the first place, the lives system was a cornerstone of shooting games since time immemorial.``
``What?  You say all that is completely obvious?``
``You're WROOONG!!  A true lives system means replaying from a fixed respawn point when you die — and nothing else!``
``Respawning on the very spot where you died may look like a lives system, but it's no different from a hit point system!``
``Who could have predicted that this cornerstone — this sacred pillar of the shooting game — would collapse at the hands of the genre's very greatest titles?!``
``Take one legendary game, which raised the golden standard for all shooters!!``
``When you die in that game, all your power-ups reset to zero, and you're sent back to a fixed respawn point — meaning...``
``In the later stages, you'd die the very instant you respawned, get rewound to the same point, and die again — over and over, endlessly.  It didn't matter how many lives you had left!``
``It's a death loop!!  That game practically invented the phrase 'death loop'!``
changeReason: Original translates ハマリ as 'trap' while new uses 'death loop' — different gaming terminology conveying a different concept.

TextENGNew: ``In response, the sequel invented something then revolutionary for the shooting genre — the on-the-spot respawn!``
``Now even beginners could relax!  Anyone could brute-force their way past any stage or boss, as long as they had lives left and a fifty-yen coin for continues!!``
``But it was at that exact moment that games lost their sanctity!``
changeReason: Original calls it 'a certain shit game' while new says 'a certain infernal game' — different characterization (クソゲー literally means 'shit game').

TextENGNew: ``The enemies you couldn't beat no matter how many times you tried!  The bosses!  The stages!!  And the rush of elation when you finally overcame them — that is Higurashi!``
``The rush of breaking free from the inescapable fate of Showa 58... it's not unlike the rush of finally clearing a certain infernal game after more than two years of attempting it!!``
``Is there any modern game that keeps the fire of conquest alive in you for that long?``
``No, there is not!!  And why?  Because anyone can just brute-force their way to the ending!!``
``No matter how sloppily you play, once you've cleared a game it loses its magic.  Weak players will never pursue the true challenge — they simply grow tired and move on!``
``In the end, it was weak gamers who brought about this age of weakness!!  And the original developers have admitted it!``
``As proof — the legitimate sequels, the true successors of that series, abandoned the on-the-spot respawn and brought back the death loop!!``
``Well... then again, for games that are just too hard, on-the-spot respawn is probably fine.``
``I mean, if you got sent back to the start of the stage every single time you died right before the mid-boss, when would you ever get to see the final boss?!``
``There was a game I played last week where I can apparently no-miss my way to the true-route final boss — except I've never actually managed to get there on my own!!``
``Please, developers — aim for a balance that both amateurs and veterans can enjoy!  Shutting the door on newcomers entirely, even if brute-force works, is just too cruel!!``
``And why do other people find hidden stages so easy to blaze through?!  What am I missing — love? Kinetic vision? Libido?!``
``Ahh, at this point I'd rather elite gamers go play in some heavenly realm instead of the local arcade!  It's fun to watch, but it makes me feel more and more pathetic!!``
``What about you, playing this right now? How do you feel about all that?!``
``Hau...  Rena has absolutely no idea what you're talking about.``
``That's a relief...  I had no idea either...``
``Really?  I understood all of it.  I always manage to see the final boss on my own skill.  Though the one that came out recently gave me a real scare!``
...I was just rambling desperately, but somehow it worked.  And it actually stopped Rena cold in her tracks.
If you're going to kill me, do it all at once.  Ask 'do you have any last words?' and there's truly no telling what'll happen.
...Ah, though this technique doesn't really work in serious scenes.  It might utterly ruin a perfectly tense, swelling, tragic moment!
``...Wait, Satoko.  He stopped Rena — but since nobody's chasing Takano, it's all for nothing.``
``Wait, what?!  Were we all just... standing here, hanging on his every word?!  Idiots, idiots — what on earth are we doing!!``
``Oh no!  Takano-san is gone!  We've lost her!!``
``Wait, Kei-chan — according to one creator, the smart bombs that wipe out everything on screen can essentially count as extra lives too—``

``No, that's because modern shmup bombs grant invincibility the instant you press the button!  In the games I played before, there was nearly a full second of lag between pressing the button and the bomb actually going off—``
``Rena doesn't really get any of this, but the little fairies that drop items when you continue are really kyuute, kyuute!``
``I have absolutely no idea what any of you are talking about.``
...Sorry, Satoko.  This might not mean anything to you, but this is something we once staked our lives on.  2D shooters, you know!  Though... we left quite a lot behind along the way.
``No, wait!  Takano-san, Takano-san!  Never mind all that — we have to go after her!!``
``O-Oh, right!!  We have to get the magatama back!``
``Kei-chan and Rika-chama — keep Rena-san busy with more of those strange conversations!!  We're going!!``
``Oh, is that so?  Have a nice time.  I wonder if something happened?  Hau.``
``Hmm?  Rena — you don't care about Takano-san anymore?!``
``...Huh?  What happened with Takano-san?  Hau.``
Rena blinked at us with a blank expression.
...Judging by her face, she didn't even seem to know why she was here.
...Which meant Takano-san must have let go of the magatama.
``...Meep — that's very possible.  If Rena is back to normal, then the white magatama no longer has a holder.``
``...Then in the worst case, it doesn't matter if nobody finds it, right?  There's no problem as long as nobody picks it up.``
``We can't leave it at that!  What if an ant picks it up by mistake and carries it back to the nest?!``
``...There'd be a world-class anthill exhibit at the Ryuugu household by the next morning.``
Anyway, we had to get Takano-san.  That runaway nurse might have flung it somewhere!!
``...And — we've finally got her.  What a lot of trouble you've caused us...``
``Ahh, geez!  I have magatama research to prepare with Rena-chan, so let me go~!!``
``Return Rika-chama's magatama before anything else!!  It's extremely important!``
...But judging by Rena's state, it seemed clear Takano-san no longer had it.
``My, that's odd...  I could have sworn I tucked it in my pocket...  How strange?``
``You're joking!!  Did you drop it somewhere?!``
I see... so that was the punchline...
Capturing Takano-san was no easy feat.
She tore all around the village with us in hot pursuit, and after quite an ordeal we finally managed to run her down.
...And somewhere in the course of all that running, she must have dropped it.
...Now all we could do was wait for some human to pick it up.
When that happened, Rena would react — and from her reaction, we'd be able to work out who the culprit was.
...But at that point, Rena would resist us with everything she had...  This is a genuine, world-class headache!!
